title: In-Hospital Outcomes Associated with Left Atrial Appendage Closure in Patients with Atrial Fibrillation and Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
text:
Study Objective: To evaluate the association between left atrial appendage closure (LAAC) and in-hospital safety outcomes and resource utilization among stable patients hospitalized electively with atrial fibrillation (AF) and heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF).
Data Source: Retrospective cohort analysis using the National Inpatient Sample (NIS) from 2018 to 2020. The NIS, part of the Healthcare Cost and Utilization Project (HCUP), is the largest all-payer inpatient care database in the United States.
Cohort Definition:

Inclusion Criteria:

Age в‰Ґ18 years.
Diagnosis of atrial fibrillation (AF) (ICD-10-CM codes I48.*).
Diagnosis of heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) (ICD-10-CM codes I502.* or I504.*).
Analysis restricted strictly to elective admissions to minimize selection bias by indication and ensure comparability between groups.

Exclusion Criteria:

HF with preserved EF or unspecified HF.
Missing documentation of LAAC status.


Exposure: Undergoing percutaneous left atrial appendage closure (LAAC) during the index hospitalization

Catheter-Based LAAC Procedures: Defined using ICD-10-PCS procedure codes for percutaneous and percutaneous-endoscopic occlusion of the left atrial appendage: 02L73DK.*, 02L73CK.*, 02L73ZK.*, 02L74DK.*, 02L74CK.*, 02L74ZK.*
This includes intraluminal devices (e.g., WatchmanВ®, AmuletВ®), extraluminal devices (e.g., LARIATВ®), and suture-based approaches.

Outcomes of Interest:

Primary Safety Outcome: In-hospital mortality.
Secondary Clinical Outcomes: In-hospital stroke (ischemic/hemorrhagic), cardiogenic shock, and major bleeding.
Resource Utilization: Length of stay (LOS) and inflation-adjusted total hospitalization charges (in 2020 USD).
Discharge Disposition: Favorable discharge (defined as routine discharge to home).

Outcome Definitions (ICD-10-CM/PCS Codes):

Stroke:

ICD-10-CM: I60.*, I61.*, I62.*, I63.*, G43601, G43609, G43611, G43619, I97810, I97811, I97820, I97821, R297.*
Encompasses ischemic stroke, hemorrhagic stroke, and procedural cerebrovascular complications.

Cardiogenic Shock:

ICD-10-CM: R570.*
Includes cardiogenic and unspecified shock.

Major Bleeding:

ICD-10-CM codes: I6[0-2].*, I31[24].*, J942.*, K661.*, I97610.*, K2[5-8][0246].*, K29(01|21).*, K31811.*, K3182.*, K5521.*, K625.*, K57..[13].*, K92[0-2].*, R58.*
A composite outcome capturing critical-site hemorrhages and gastrointestinal bleeding requiring transfusion.

Favorable Discharge:

Defined as routine discharge to home, identified using the NIS DISPUNIFORM variable.


Covariates & Risk Stratification:

Baseline Risk: A modified CHAв‚‚DSв‚‚-VASc score was calculated for each patient using ICD-10 codes.
Comorbidities: Elixhauser Comorbidity Index (weighted sum).
Demographics/Hospital: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, payer, income quartile, hospital region, bed size, and teaching status.

Statistical Methods (Propensity Weighting):

Univariable Analysis:

Categorical variables: Compared using PearsonвЂ™s chi-square test with Rao & Scott adjustment
Continuous variables: Analyzed using the design-based KruskalвЂ“Wallis test, a non-parametric alternative suitable for survey-weighted data

Propensity Weighted Multivariable Analysis:

To account for non-random treatment assignment, we employed Inverse Probability of Treatment Weighting (IPTW) based on propensity scores.
Propensity scores were estimated using a logistic regression model including all baseline covariates and the modified CHAв‚‚DSв‚‚-VASc score.
Balance was assessed using standardized mean differences (SMD).
Outcomes were analyzed using survey-weighted regression models (logistic for binary, linear for continuous) incorporating the IPTW weights to estimate the Average Treatment Effect (ATE). Results are reported as adjusted odds ratios (aOR) or beta coefficients with 95% confidence intervals.


Software: All statistical analyses were performed using the R Statistical Language (Version 4.5.2; R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria), incorporating survey-weighted procedures via the survey package to account for the complex sampling design of NIS.

Inclusion Criteria:

Age в‰Ґ18 years.
Diagnosis of atrial fibrillation (AF) (ICD-10-CM codes I48.*).
Diagnosis of heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) (ICD-10-CM codes I502.* or I504.*).
Analysis restricted strictly to elective admissions to minimize selection bias by indication and ensure comparability between groups.

This includes intraluminal devices (e.g., WatchmanВ®, AmuletВ®), extraluminal devices (e.g., LARIATВ®), and suture-based approaches.

Encompasses ischemic stroke, hemorrhagic stroke, and procedural cerebrovascular complications.

Cardiogenic Shock:

ICD-10-CM: R570.*
Includes cardiogenic and unspecified shock.

Major Bleeding:

ICD-10-CM codes: I6[0-2].*, I31[24].*, J942.*, K661.*, I97610.*, K2[5-8][0246].*, K29(01|21).*, K31811.*, K3182.*, K5521.*, K625.*, K57..[13].*, K92[0-2].*, R58.*

Baseline Risk: A modified CHAв‚‚DSв‚‚-VASc score was calculated for each patient using ICD-10 codes.

Demographics/Hospital: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, payer, income quartile, hospital region, bed size, and teaching status.

To account for non-random treatment assignment, we employed Inverse Probability of Treatment Weighting (IPTW) based on propensity scores.
Propensity scores were estimated using a logistic regression model including all baseline covariates and the modified CHAв‚‚DSв‚‚-VASc score.

Software: All statistical analyses were performed using the R Statistical Language (Version 4.5.2; R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria), incorporating survey-weighted procedures via the survey package to account for the complex sampling design of NIS.
